headline: 7 token contract addresses catalogued across Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Polygon; additional chains (Monad, Plasma, Celo, Ink) not yet resolved

findings.text: Etherscan block-explorer search surfaced 2 Ethereum addresses: current proxy 0x68749665ff8d2d112fa859aa293f07a622782f38 and old token 0x4922a015c4407f87432b179bb209e125432e4a2a (migrated)
Arbiscan name-tag search and news sources confirmed Arbitrum token proxy 0x40461291347e1ecbb09499f3371d3f17f10d7159 with implementation at 0x9001dbe4d68d36ab87923a2a9dfb0c745fd25001
Polygon block-explorer confirmed token proxy 0xf1815bd50389c46847f0bda824ec8da914045d14 with implementation at 0x6d205337f45d6850c3c3006e28d5b52c8a432c35
Protocol website at gold.tether.to returns JavaScript-only interface; no deployed contracts documentation page discovered
No audit links recorded in protocol metadata; no audit reports discovered via search
No GitHub repository pinned in protocol metadata; repository and deployment artifacts not discovered
D5: chain Monad: zero addresses surfaced from explorer and search sources
D5: chain Plasma: zero addresses surfaced from explorer and search sources
D5: chain Celo: zero addresses surfaced from explorer and search sources
D5: chain Ink: zero addresses surfaced from explorer and search sources
Addresses classified as token (proxy and implementation), with proxy_admin inferred from proxy contract pattern (admin address not yet read from storage)

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 7 contract addresses (3 token proxies, 3 implementations, 1 creator EOA) across Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Polygon. Deployed contracts documentation and GitHub sources not accessible via website. Four chains listed in protocol.chains remain unresolved (Monad, Plasma, Celo, Ink). Ethereum contracts use Transparent Upgradeable Proxy pattern; Arbitrum and Polygon use LayerZero OFT-based token pattern. Next run should inherit discovered addresses and attempt admin-address enumeration.

evidence.shows: Ethereum mainnet token proxy at 0x68749665ff8d2d112fa859aa293f07a622782f38, Transparent Upgradeable Proxy pattern, implementation at 0x4c0d2c74a8d26f1e4f5653021c521f5471f9e566, deployed by 0x341a38b69a6b8f00fad4eed3a2b8528e3fed07ff
Old Ethereum XAUt token contract (migrated), token created 2020-01-08, deployed by Will Harborne via Bitfinex, migration notice present

Arbitrum Mainnet XAUt0 token proxy at 0x40461291347e1ecbb09499f3371d3f17f10d7159, ERC-20 Proxy, implementation at 0x9001dbe4...45fd25001 (TetherTokenOFTExtension), uses EIP-1967 Transparent Proxy pattern

Polygon Mainnet XAUt0 token proxy at 0xf1815bd50389c46847f0bda824ec8da914045d14, ERC-20 Proxy, implementation at 0x6d205337f45d6850c3c3006e28d5b52c8a432c35 (TetherTokenOFTExtension), uses EIP-1967 Transparent Proxy pattern
Tether Gold protocol overview: RWA token backed 1:1 by physical gold in London Good Delivery bars, launched 2020 by TG Commodities Limited
XAUt launched on Ethereum (ERC-20), then expanded to TRON and Arbitrum using LayerZero for cross-chain interoperability; ToN and Hyperliquid integrations mentioned
Arbitrum marked XAUt0's 4th supporting blockchain (after Polygon, Hyperliquid, TON); LayerZero OFT infrastructure; launch October 2025

unknowns: D2: No deployed contracts / addresses documentation page found on gold.tether.to or linked docs
D3: No audit reports or links discovered; protocol may be unaudited
D4: No GitHub repository pinned or discovered; deployment artifacts (Foundry/Hardhat) not accessible
D5: chain Monad: zero addresses surfaced
D5: chain Plasma: zero addresses surfaced
D5: chain Celo: zero addresses surfaced
D5: chain Ink: zero addresses surfaced
D6: LayerZero OFT factory enumeration not attempted; child contracts not catalogued
D8: Proxy admin addresses for Arbitrum and Polygon not yet read from EIP-1967 storage slots

protocol_metadata.about: Tether Gold (XAUt) is a commodity-backed stablecoin providing digital ownership of physical gold stored in secure Swiss vaults. Each token represents one fine troy ounce of gold meeting London Bullion Market Association Good Delivery standard. Launched in January 2020 by TG Commodities Limited, it combines the stability of physical gold with blockchain portability and divisibility, enabling 24/7 trading and direct redemption for qualified users.
